summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a stellar early‑68 show with a debut lyrical The Other One, fiery New Potato/Schoolgirl sequence and an electrifying Eleven finale.
review: The Miller‑upgraded SBD delivers remarkably clean audio—only faint hiss remains—letting the band’s tight early‑’68 performance shine. Highlights include the debut lyric version of The Other One, a blazing New Potato segue into Schoolgirl, and an Eleven that rockets into feedback, while Jerry Garcia’s bluesy leads on It Hurts Me Too and a concise Dark Star add extra sparkle.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews are 5‑star, praising both the performance (standout songs, tight playing) and the recording (great mix, minimal hiss). A few lower ratings note minor cuts but do not detract from overall excellence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Very clean mix after Miller's cleanup; only a touch of hiss and occasional tape‑cut glitches (Cryptical reprise, Dark Star start) but overall crisp with zero audience bleed.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Early set builds with a powerful New Potato/Schoolgirl transition; the second half closes strongly with an Eleven that launches into feedback, delivering a tight, energetic early‑68 performance.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s leads are highlighted throughout—bluesy work on It Hurts Me Too, sick leads in the China/Eleven jam, and a concise yet powerful Dark Star.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass shines especially on Born Cross‑Eyed where reviewers note a cool Phil ending.
band_member_comments.Bob: Pigpen’s harmonica is praised on Schoolgirl and the “nasty” break on It Hurts Me Too.
